title: Roblox Q4 2024 Sales Top Estimates But Stock Drops 21.5%
text: Roblox delivered strong Q4 2024 sales that topped analyst estimates, yet the stock declined 21.5% in response. Revenue of $988.2 million beat expectations and represented 32% year-over-year growth. Bookings of $1.36 billion also exceeded estimates. Despite the beat, investors sold the stock aggressively, suggesting forward guidance disappointment. The 21.5% post-earnings decline illustrated how guidance outlooks dominated near-term stock performance relative to actual results. Daily active users of 82.9 million continued strong growth momentum. Free cash flow surged 417% to $641.3 million, demonstrating improving cash generation. Management provided guidance for 2025 that reflected continued strong growth but perhaps below peak expectations. The disconnect between beating current period estimates and declining stock prices suggested market focus on decelerating growth rates rather than absolute performance. Adjusted EBITDA of $65.6 million showed the company was making progress toward profitability. The stock decline despite strong results underscored investor concern about growth trajectory sustainability.
